core_payload.content:
# Workspace

## 1. Role
Workspace provides the operator composition layer that makes N1Hub behave like one coherent habitat while keeping deeper module contracts explicit. It routes that habitat through five bounded sub-hubs so visibility, execution state, assistant control, identity governance, and runtime lanes remain legible at higher scale.

## 2. Routing Surfaces
- `operator-surfaces` exposes cross-module visibility, time pressure, health signals, and review context in one calm cockpit.
- `execution-loop` composes projects, plans, and tracker state into one accountable execution field.
- `assistant-control` carries assistant session state, chat-to-capsule handoffs, wallet posture, and delegation controls.
- `identity-access` defines profile, team, entitlement, and integration governance.
- `runtime-lanes` exposes long-lived execution substrates that workspace supervises without semantically owning.

## 3. Parent Boundary
The parent workspace hub keeps only the top-level habitat relationship to N1Hub, CapsuleOS, the five sub-hubs, To Dig Deep, hub-and-atomic doctrine, and the enclosing workspace project. Detailed module routing flows through the sub-hubs so workspace stays a composition lens instead of a flat registry.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Workspace is the future parent hub for five bounded composition surfaces: operator surfaces, execution loop, assistant control, identity access, and runtime lanes. The parent keeps only top-level habitat relationships while detailed module routing flows through sub-hubs, which makes cross-module visibility, execution state, assistant control, and runtime supervision easier to inspect without turning workspace into a flat registry of leaves. This keeps the operator habitat coherent, legible, and reviewable even as the platform grows more capable.

This aligns with the decomposition goal to clarify and reduce complexity while preserving architectural intent.

That supports centering the summary on hub responsibilities and recording decomposition around module linkage, state visibility, and topology enforcement without rewriting the underlying doctrine.

Real absorbed the workspace boundary doctrine that now belongs in canonical form. Dream was rewritten to keep only future-facing operator composition delta and to stop restating the stronger real-side runtime inventory.
Stripped meta-commentary leakage. Dream now asserts future state directly.
Synced Dream parent routing to the live five-subhub workspace topology so future doctrine no longer collapses execution, assistant, identity, and runtime concerns into one flat hub.
